notes: Mississippi received $205.9M for FY2026. MAJOR UPDATE (April 29, 2026): Governor Tate Reeves established the Mississippi Rural Health Transformation Program (RHTP) Office within the Office of the Governor and launched the official program website at mississippirhtp.com. Richard Grimes (CPA with extensive healthcare finance/policy experience) named Project Director. The new RHTP Office serves as the central hub for coordinating implementation of the State's approved plan, ensuring alignment across agencies, managing program administration, and maintaining a strong focus on accountability and results. Mississippi's plan is now organized into FIVE KEY INITIATIVES: (1) CRIS - Coordinated Regional Integrated Systems (regional EMS coordination, treat in place, post-discharge care, 24/7 nurse navigation, AI decision support, social services integration, 911 system integration); (2) WEI - Workforce Expansion Initiative; (3) HTAM - Health Technology Advancement & Modernization (statewide health information exchange); (4) TAPS - Telehealth Adoption & Provider Support; (5) BRIDGE - Building Rural Infrastructure for Delivery, Growth & Efficiency. Each initiative page on mississippirhtp.com states: 'Notices of Funding Opportunities (NOFOs) will be released soon.' Eligible organizations: rural hospitals, critical access hospitals, community health centers, behavioral health providers, EMS services, and community-based organizations serving rural Mississippi. All funding must align with Mississippi's Rural Health Transformation Plan, address specific care gaps, support system-level improvements (not isolated projects), and comply with federal funding regulations. HISTORICAL: HB1067 (competitive bidding) went to conference committee but the legislature adjourned sine die (~April 5) before it could be resolved - effectively dead. Governor Reeves VETOED companion bill SB 2477 (reporting/oversight requirements) on April 3, 2026, citing risk of losing up to $1 billion in RHTP funding over 5 years. The governor's office retains full discretion over RHTP vendor contract awards; competitive bidding is NOT legislatively required. BDO Government Services (formerly HORNE) confirmed as consulting/admin vendor (selected January 2026). Governor signed separate bill March 24 easing CON requirements for ~55 rural hospitals to expand services. Statewide assessment of rural health needs projected complete by September 2026. RFQ posted (RFx# 3120003272) for accounting, auditing, and third-party administration consulting services for RHTP oversight/compliance. Six initiatives originally planned. Contact: info@mississippirhtp.com or via the website contact form. NOTE: A May 1-June 1, 2026 $25M RHTP application opportunity widely cited online belongs to PENNSYLVANIA (Department of Human Services), NOT Mississippi. Mississippi has not yet opened any RHTP applications to vendors/providers.

notes: Wyoming received $205.0M for FY2026 ($5M more than requested, second-largest per-capita award behind Alaska). HB 122 SIGNED INTO LAW by Governor Gordon on March 6, 2026. The bill establishes the WY RHTP, creates advisory committee (9 voting members appointed by governor), perpetuity fund (80% of Year 1 funds), and expenditure account. Perpetuity fund allocations: CAH Basic Incentive (40.7%), EMS Regionalization (26.9%), Workforce Education (21.6%), Physician Post-Graduate Medical Education (10.8%). Years 2-5: 69.5% to perpetuity fund. Annual distribution: greater of prior-year program income or 4% of total accumulated fund assets. CMS APPROVAL STILL UNCERTAIN: WDH Director Johansson says CMS 'formally approved' the perpetuity fund concept in a December call, but CMS has not publicly confirmed this. Emails obtained by KFF Health News show CMS told some states in early November that grant money cannot 'fund an endowment, capital fund, or other vehicle resembling an investment fund.' If CMS and WDH cannot reach agreement, a special legislative session may be required (per Sen. Mike Gierau). Six of 30 Wyoming hospitals reportedly in immediate jeopardy of closing. Legislature budget session adjourned March 11. UPDATE (early April 2026): As of early April, CMS had not approved or had only partially approved Wyoming's state budget; CMS spokesperson Catherine Howden declined to identify which states still needed revised budgets approved. Wyoming Legislature's Management Council met April 1 and approved interim topics with the RHTP perpetuity fund as a major focus. Per WDH timeline on health.wyo.gov: RFAs/RFPs are scheduled to be posted between May and June 2026, with informational webinars and Q&A sessions to precede those postings. NO RFPs/RFAs RELEASED YET as of May 3, 2026 - WDH still negotiating implementation details with CMS. Monitor health.wyo.gov RHTP page.
